speaker: CHAMBERLAIN
text: I fear he will indeed; well, let him have them.

speaker: CHAMBERLAIN
text: He will have all, I think.

speaker: NORFOLK
text: Well met, my Lord Chamberlain.

speaker: CHAMBERLAIN
text: Good day to both your Graces.

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: How is the King employed?

speaker: CHAMBERLAIN
text: I left him private,

speaker: CHAMBERLAIN
text: Full of sad thoughts and troubles.

speaker: NORFOLK
text: What’s the cause?

speaker: CHAMBERLAIN
text: It seems the marriage with his brother’s wife

speaker: CHAMBERLAIN
text: Has crept too near his conscience.

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: No, his conscience

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: Has crept too near another lady.

speaker: NORFOLK
text: ’Tis so;

speaker: NORFOLK
text: This is the Cardinal’s doing. The king-cardinal,

speaker: NORFOLK
text: That blind priest, like the eldest son of Fortune,

speaker: NORFOLK
text: Turns what he list. The King will know him one day.

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: Pray God he do! He’ll never know himself else.

speaker: NORFOLK
text: How holily he works in all his business,

speaker: NORFOLK
text: And with what zeal! For, now he has cracked the league

speaker: NORFOLK
text: Between us and the Emperor, the Queen’s great-nephew,

speaker: NORFOLK
text: He dives into the King’s soul and there scatters

speaker: NORFOLK
text: Dangers, doubts, wringing of the conscience,

speaker: NORFOLK
text: Fears and despairs—and all these for his marriage.

speaker: NORFOLK
text: And out of all these to restore the King,

speaker: NORFOLK
text: He counsels a divorce, a loss of her

speaker: NORFOLK
text: That like a jewel has hung twenty years

speaker: NORFOLK
text: About his neck, yet never lost her luster;

speaker: NORFOLK
text: Of her that loves him with that excellence

speaker: NORFOLK
text: That angels love good men with; even of her

speaker: NORFOLK
text: That, when the greatest stroke of fortune falls,

speaker: NORFOLK
text: Will bless the King. And is not this course pious?

speaker: CHAMBERLAIN
text: Heaven keep me from such counsel! ’Tis most true:

speaker: CHAMBERLAIN
text: These news are everywhere, every tongue speaks ’em,

speaker: CHAMBERLAIN
text: And every true heart weeps for ’t. All that dare

speaker: CHAMBERLAIN
text: Look into these affairs see this main end,

speaker: CHAMBERLAIN
text: The French king’s sister. Heaven will one day open

speaker: CHAMBERLAIN
text: The King’s eyes, that so long have slept upon

speaker: CHAMBERLAIN
text: This bold bad man.

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: And free us from his slavery.

speaker: NORFOLK
text: We had need pray,

speaker: NORFOLK
text: And heartily, for our deliverance,

speaker: NORFOLK
text: Or this imperious man will work us all

speaker: NORFOLK
text: From princes into pages. All men’s honors

speaker: NORFOLK
text: Lie like one lump before him, to be fashioned

speaker: NORFOLK
text: Into what pitch he please.

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: For me, my lords,

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: I love him not nor fear him; there’s my creed.

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: As I am made without him, so I’ll stand,

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: If the King please. His curses and his blessings

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: Touch me alike: they’re breath I not believe in.

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: I knew him and I know him; so I leave him

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: To him that made him proud, the Pope.

speaker: NORFOLK
text: Let’s in,

speaker: NORFOLK
text: And with some other business put the King

speaker: NORFOLK
text: From these sad thoughts that work too much upon him.—

speaker: NORFOLK
text: My lord, you’ll bear us company?

speaker: CHAMBERLAIN
text: Excuse me;

speaker: CHAMBERLAIN
text: The King has sent me otherwhere. Besides,

speaker: CHAMBERLAIN
text: You’ll find a most unfit time to disturb him.

speaker: CHAMBERLAIN
text: Health to your Lordships.

speaker: NORFOLK
text: Thanks, my good Lord Chamberlain.

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: How sad he looks! Sure he is much afflicted.

speaker: KING
text: Who’s there? Ha?

speaker: NORFOLK
text: Pray God he be not angry.

speaker: KING
text: Who’s there, I say? How dare you thrust yourselves

speaker: KING
text: Into my private meditations? Who am I, ha?

speaker: NORFOLK
text: A gracious king that pardons all offenses

speaker: NORFOLK
text: Malice ne’er meant. Our breach of duty this way

speaker: NORFOLK
text: Is business of estate, in which we come

speaker: NORFOLK
text: To know your royal pleasure.

speaker: KING
text: You are too bold.

speaker: KING
text: Go to; I’ll make you know your times of business.

speaker: KING
text: Is this an hour for temporal affairs, ha?

speaker: KING
text: Who’s there? My good Lord Cardinal? O my Wolsey,

speaker: KING
text: The quiet of my wounded conscience,

speaker: KING
text:
Thou art a cure fit for a king.
                 
                To
                 
                Campeius
                .
               You’re welcome,

speaker: KING
text: Most learnèd reverend sir, into our kingdom.

speaker: KING
text: Use us and it.—My good lord, have great care

speaker: KING
text: I be not found a talker.

speaker: WOLSEY
text: Sir, you cannot.

speaker: WOLSEY
text: I would your Grace would give us but an hour

speaker: WOLSEY
text: Of private conference.

speaker: KING
text: We are busy. Go.

speaker: NORFOLK
text: This priest has no pride in him?

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: Not to speak of.

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: I would not be so sick, though for his place.

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: But this cannot continue.

speaker: NORFOLK
text: If it do,

speaker: NORFOLK
text: I’ll venture one have-at-him.

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: I another.

speaker: WOLSEY
text: Your Grace has given a precedent of wisdom

speaker: WOLSEY
text: Above all princes in committing freely

speaker: WOLSEY
text: Your scruple to the voice of Christendom.

speaker: WOLSEY
text: Who can be angry now? What envy reach you?

speaker: WOLSEY
text: The Spaniard, tied by blood and favor to her,

speaker: WOLSEY
text: Must now confess, if they have any goodness,

speaker: WOLSEY
text: The trial just and noble; all the clerks—

speaker: WOLSEY
text: I mean the learnèd ones in Christian kingdoms—

speaker: WOLSEY
text: Have their free voices; Rome, the nurse of judgment,

speaker: WOLSEY
text: Invited by your noble self, hath sent

speaker: WOLSEY
text: One general tongue unto us, this good man,

speaker: WOLSEY
text: This just and learnèd priest, Cardinal Campeius,

speaker: WOLSEY
text: Whom once more I present unto your Highness.

speaker: KING
text: And once more in mine arms I bid him welcome,

speaker: KING
text: And thank the holy conclave for their loves.

speaker: KING
text: They have sent me such a man I would have wished for.

speaker: CAMPEIUS
text: Your Grace must needs deserve all strangers’ loves,

speaker: CAMPEIUS
text: You are so noble. To your Highness’ hand

speaker: CAMPEIUS
text: I tender my commission—by whose virtue,

speaker: CAMPEIUS
text: The court of Rome commanding, you, my Lord

speaker: CAMPEIUS
text: Cardinal of York, are joined with me their servant

speaker: CAMPEIUS
text: In the unpartial judging of this business.

speaker: KING
text: Two equal men. The Queen shall be acquainted

speaker: KING
text: Forthwith for what you come. Where’s Gardiner?

speaker: WOLSEY
text: I know your Majesty has always loved her

speaker: WOLSEY
text: So dear in heart not to deny her that

speaker: WOLSEY
text: A woman of less place might ask by law:

speaker: WOLSEY
text: Scholars allowed freely to argue for her.

speaker: KING
text: Ay, and the best she shall have, and my favor

speaker: KING
text: To him that does best. God forbid else. Cardinal,

speaker: KING
text: Prithee call Gardiner to me, my new secretary.

speaker: KING
text: I find him a fit fellow.

speaker: WOLSEY
text: Give me your hand. Much joy and favor to you.

speaker: WOLSEY
text: You are the King’s now.

speaker: GARDINER
text: But to be commanded

speaker: GARDINER
text: Forever by your Grace, whose hand has raised me.

speaker: KING
text: Come hither, Gardiner.

speaker: CAMPEIUS
text: My lord of York, was not one Doctor Pace

speaker: CAMPEIUS
text: In this man’s place before him?

speaker: WOLSEY
text: Yes, he was.

speaker: CAMPEIUS
text: Was he not held a learnèd man?

speaker: WOLSEY
text: Yes, surely.

speaker: CAMPEIUS
text: Believe me, there’s an ill opinion spread, then,

speaker: CAMPEIUS
text: Even of yourself, Lord Cardinal.

speaker: WOLSEY
text: How? Of me?

speaker: CAMPEIUS
text: They will not stick to say you envied him

speaker: CAMPEIUS
text: And, fearing he would rise—he was so virtuous—

speaker: CAMPEIUS
text: Kept him a foreign man still, which so grieved him

speaker: CAMPEIUS
text: That he ran mad and died.

speaker: WOLSEY
text: Heav’n’s peace be with him!

speaker: WOLSEY
text: That’s Christian care enough. For living murmurers,

speaker: WOLSEY
text: There’s places of rebuke. He was a fool,

speaker: WOLSEY
text: For he would needs be virtuous. That good fellow

speaker: WOLSEY
text: If I command him follows my appointment.

speaker: WOLSEY
text: I will have none so near else. Learn this, brother:

speaker: WOLSEY
text: We live not to be griped by meaner persons.

speaker: KING
text: Deliver this with modesty to th’ Queen.

speaker: KING
text: The most convenient place that I can think of

speaker: KING
text: For such receipt of learning is Blackfriars.

speaker: KING
text: There you shall meet about this weighty business.

speaker: KING
text: My Wolsey, see it furnished. O, my lord,

speaker: KING
text: Would it not grieve an able man to leave

speaker: KING
text: So sweet a bedfellow? But, conscience, conscience!

speaker: KING
text: O, ’tis a tender place, and I must leave her.
